title: phlegmariurus subfalciformis
intro: Phlegmariurus subfalciformis, also known as the forked forked polypody, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Phlegmariurus subfalciformis include the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as the relief of fever, headaches, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Phlegmariurus subfalciformis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
